title: The Nature of the Ghostly Reis Rei Lilith GNR and all that
display_title: The Nature of the Ghostly Reis Rei Lilith GNR and all that
wikitext:
[]: The Nature of the Ghostly Reis: Rei/Lilith/GNR and all that             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sat Jan 02, 2010 7:39 pm I wanted to start a thread on something that occurred to me during another discussion on the EML. It involves the nature of the connections between Rei, Lilith, GNR and all the various aspects of Rei throughout the series. In particular, I wanted to discuss the nature of the silent, almost ghostlike Rei apparitions that are seen in episode #01 and in the End of Evangelion, particular the "bookend" appearances at the beginning of #01 and the end of #26'. I'd like to get peoples opinions on the main speculation I'm going to make in the second post, but before that, I'd better mention some preliminary details and lay some needed groundwork in this post. For the sake of brevity here, I'll dub this Ghostly Rei and all her appearances Rei 0 . Moreover because I wish to make a distinction between them, I'll dub the Lilith on the cross in Terminal Dogma Lilith-0 , and the completed Lilith after fusion with Rei III Lilith-1 . The original Lilith before Rei's extraction I'll dub Lilith-SoL . (for the original Source of life). Before going further, I better clear up an important position I'm holding here. That is, the relationship between the three incarnations Rei I,II and III (as well as the soul in Unit-00) and Lilith-0 hanging on the cross in Terminal Dogma. By now the theory of "soul/spirit division" is well established as an explanation of how the "soul" of Rei/Lilith seemingly exists in several entities. Going by this premise, Rei I, II and III do not entirely contain the soul of the second angel, but instead only some piece or shard (perhaps the largest) of the greater whole that once was Lilith-SoL, the second angel. Each is in turn the Rei "prime", the most conscious aspect of the divided Lilith-SoL, with Lilith-0 itself and the possibly the Reiquarium bodies being much smaller and less conscious pieces of the whole. However, a key position I would hold here is that _all_ the mysterious Rei's we see in these episodes are in fact the same Rei 0. In fact, this conclusion is rather inescapable; there is a clear visual and narrative link between the various Rei 0's, especially in #25' and #26'. Unfortunately, this seems to require us to postulate either Rei 0 being able to travel though time or being in some sense "acausal", which is precisely what is proposed in the standard interpretation. This is justified by appealing the the many references to quantum mechanics in the show and by appealing to the strange acasual nature of quantum particles*. While this is speculative, it must be stressed that Rei 0's are undeniably linked, so they must be linked somehow. The standard interpretation of all the Rei 0 appearances relates to the powers that Rei gains upon uniting with Lilith-0(and Kaworu) to become an Lilith-1. This interpretation states that the Rei 0's are all a manifestation of, or an avatar of, this now God-like being(GNR could be another) and their supernatural abilities, including their acausality, stem from this state. I'm not going to stray too far from this interpretation, but I'd like to present an alternative view the conclusion of acauality. * In Feynman's sum-over-histories model, a quantum particle moves from point A to point B by travelling across all possible paths from A to B, in space as well as time. The "sum" of all these paths works to to be—approximately—the path you would expect the particle to take. This has been you daily dose of phun-physics-facts.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sat Jan 02, 2010 7:40 pm Now, postulating time travel to explain the Rei 0 appearances is perhaps a stretch, and some on the EML raised several objections to it, which got me to think about the subject again. So instead of assuming some form of acausality, I'd like to make some speculations on the nature of Rei 0. Firstly, some details about Rei 0 need to be examined. One item that caught my eye as I was going through the commentary cuts again was the following quote from the Platinium commentaries Platinium Commentaries Episode #01 wrote: When Shinji comes to New Tokyo 3, he sees a girl that seems to be Rei Ayanami for just an instant. Considering how she is injured and wrapped up in bandages, when he later meets her in NERV Headquarters, the natural assumption would be that this was a phantom vision. But in Episode 26', "My True Heart For You," a different possibility is suggested. The girl that appeared for just one cut in this scene may be the Rei Ayanami who is " the existence that gazes upon man ."

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Jan 02, 2010 8:15 pm By now the theory of "soul/spirit division" is well established as an explanation of how the "soul" of Rei/Lilith seemingly exists in several entities. Going by this premise, Rei I, II and III do not entirely contain the soul of the second angel, but instead only some piece or shard (perhaps the largest) of the greater whole that once was Lilith-SoL, the second angel. Each is in turn the Rei "prime", the most conscious aspect of the divided Lilith-SoL, with Lilith-0 itself and the possibly the Reiquarium bodies being much smaller and less conscious pieces of the whole.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sat Jan 02, 2010 8:22 pm Well, here's another topic on which I've done lots of musings on all by myself (I resent all time travel theories and thus all recent Discussion topics on subject have been of no interest) that gets suddenly brought up. short answer that covers absolutely everything and every single mystifying piece of lore that are hard to fit together otherwise: Lilith became Goddess (and I'm talking about really divine being, not some wishywashy creature with godlike power) more on that tomorrow. Me = sleepy

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sat Jan 02, 2010 8:38 pm I really like this analysis and interpretation OMF; I especially love how you link Lilith's gaze to the line in the commentary. That's something I never noticed or considered. I remember once stating that I felt the ghostly Reis represented the "mothers" (including Lilith) watching Shinji. They represent a kind of circular philosophy that's ever present in the series - death & rebirth and whatnot. It makes sense that we first see her at "the beginning" of the series where she acts like a transition guide into the world of the series, we see her again when she acts as a transition guide into the "death" or world of Instrumentality, and we see her for a last time at the end of the series which, in itself, represents a rebirth and a "new beginning". Rei/Lilith/Yui's ties to the Goddess archetype are the strongest in the series and I think it's reasonable to assume that when they finally all converge in EoE they have achieved a kind of transcendent "God" status.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat Jan 02, 2010 8:46 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Now, one problem with this argument is that if Rei 0 is the avatar of Lilith-0, then why is she again used so prominently as the avatar of Lilith-1 later in #26'?

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sat Jan 02, 2010 8:53 pm Reminds me of the idea that we all exist because God is watching us (I believe it was a theologian named Berkeley who put that forward first). They all exist because Rei-0/Lilith-0 is watching them. By changing the very nature of Lilith-0, the existence that watches over humanity, to Rei-0, they change humanity itself. We are what we are in the eyes of those observing us. Does that make any sense, or does that obfuscate things unnecessarily?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Jan 02, 2010 9:20 pm The only real comment I have to say is that "Rei O" as god(ess) is indeed watching over Shinji and help his journey along the way. Not only she's bookend Rei as well as those various Reis seen in EoE, but I strongly believe that she's hell-train Rei/"Lilith O" of the episode 16/19/20/26' Hell-train motif, although in EoE she's represented as Rei 3, which makes sense considering Rei 3 is the clone to return to Lilith and such. Other then that, I think anything else I commented about this theory was in EML, so I don't have much to add right now. Solid analysis however.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Jan 02, 2010 9:37 pm Hmm. I still prefer the 'quantum Rei steps beyond time' idea. The visual links are a god find, but they IMO they only serve to reinforce the Rei/Lilith connection in general.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sat Jan 02, 2010 9:43 pm AuraTwilight wrote: I'll admit, your Rei-0 = Lilith-0 idea has some elegance to it, as it does away with the time travel that gives so many people so many problems, but I can't help having a gripe here; if we take out the time travel aspect, to what relevance is all the Quantum Physics stuff dropped in the narrative in relation to Rei's creation?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Jan 02, 2010 10:59 pm I agree with this point, but perhaps other explanations are possible. It may not be entirely impossible that Lilith-0 was aware Shinji's importance and kept a watchful eye on him, even communicated with him as sort of a guide to get him to where he was in EoE. This theory may suggest that Lilith had planned for the events in EoE to occur.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sat Jan 02, 2010 11:27 pm AuraTwilight wrote: That almost requires Lilith to have precognitive powers or something, which is JUST as problematic as the time travel thing, if not moreso. ........ I guess it works, on a meta-level, but Shinji himself saw that girl, and thus it was something acknowledged within the narrative. Therefore we need to have an in-narrative reason for why Rei was watching him; there's no way she would know Shinji was important without appealing to a supernatural knowledge of future events.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Jan 03, 2010 12:01 am I still think it's far more likely Rei (is it 4 or 5 now?) just stepped out of time at the end of EoE and thus is free to go whenever she pleases. Too bad she only ever seems to watch Shinji. It would have been pretty interesting if other people had Rei sightings... Gendo, perhaps.

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Sun Jan 03, 2010 12:12 am OMF wrote: NemZ wrote: The visual links are a god find, but they IMO they only serve to reinforce the Rei/Lilith connection in general.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Jan 03, 2010 12:17 am NemZ wrote: Too bad she only ever seems to watch Shinji. It would have been pretty interesting if other people had Rei sightings... Gendo, perhaps.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Jan 03, 2010 12:34 am ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: I believe the role of Rei as a "watcher" an important part of her character and is key to the connection between Rei 0 and Lilith 0. This is in fact, the lynchpin of the entire proposal. The "mother" aspect of Lilith could also give motivation for he watchings.

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Sun Jan 03, 2010 12:59 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: Wasn't one of the widely accepted interpretations of the Shinji/Sandbox scene that the sun represented the mother watching Shinji? I was also going to propose that there's a scene that links Rei with a glaring sun during one of the hell train sequences (where it obscures her face) but I don't recall if that was Rei or the younger Shinji.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Jan 03, 2010 1:00 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: I was also going to propose that there's a scene that links Rei with a glaring sun during one of the hell train sequences (where it obscures her face) but I don't recall if that was Rei or the younger Shinji.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Jan 03, 2010 1:08 am Merridian wrote: Equating the sun during the sandbox scene as representative of 'mother' doesn't make much sense to me, for pretty much the same reason; Mother's already been associated with 'Moon', so why would there be a sudden polar reversal?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Jan 03, 2010 2:24 am Sachi wrote: Eva Yojimbo wrote: I was also going to propose that there's a scene that links Rei with a glaring sun during one of the hell train sequences (where it obscures her face) but I don't recall if that was Rei or the younger Shinji.
